authors_text: Qisheng Wang,Ming Yang,Xinrui Zhu
title: Double-Ended Palindromic Trees in Linear Time
title_zh: 线性时间的双端回文树
comment: Full version, 64 pages, 2 tables, 17 algorithms. Title changed, abstract improved, some proofs simplified, the persistent part removed for simplicity
comments: Full version, 64 pages, 2 tables, 17 algorithms. Title changed, abstract improved, some proofs simplified, the persistent part removed for simplicity
abstract: The palindromic tree (a.k.a. eertree) is a data structure that provides access to all palindromic substrings of a string. In this paper, we propose a dynamic version of eertree, called double-ended eertree, which supports online operations on the stored string, including double-ended queue operations, counting distinct palindromic substrings, and finding the longest palindromic prefix/suffix. At the heart of our construction, we identify a new class of substring occurrences, called surfaces, that are palindromic substring occurrences that are neither prefixes nor suffixes of any other palindromic substring occurrences, which is of independent interest. Surfaces characterize the link structure of all palindromic substrings in the eertree, thereby allowing a linear-time implementation of double-ended eertrees through a linear-time maintenance of surfaces.
summary: The palindromic tree (a.k.a. eertree) is a data structure that provides access to all palindromic substrings of a string. In this paper, we propose a dynamic version of eertree, called double-ended eertree, which supports online operations on the stored string, including double-ended queue operations, counting distinct palindromic substrings, and finding the longest palindromic prefix/suffix. At the heart of our construction, we identify a new class of substring occurrences, called surfaces, that are palindromic substring occurrences that are neither prefixes nor suffixes of any other palindromic substring occurrences, which is of independent interest. Surfaces characterize the link structure of all palindromic substrings in the eertree, thereby allowing a linear-time implementation of double-ended eertrees through a linear-time maintenance of surfaces.
abstract_zh: 回文树（又名 eertree）是一种数据结构，提供对字符串的所有回文子字符串的访问。在本文中，我们提出了一种动态版本的 eertree，称为双端 eertree，它支持对存储字符串的在线操作，包括双端队列操作、计算不同回文子串以及查找最长回文前缀/后缀。在我们构建的核心，我们确定了一类新的子串出现，称为表面，它们是回文子串出现，既不是任何其他回文子串出现的前缀也不是后缀，这是独立的兴趣。表面表征了 eertree 中所有回文子串的链接结构，从而允许通过表面的线性时间维护来线性时间实现双端 eertree。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种动态版本的回文树（称为双端eertree），支持对存储字符串进行在线操作，并通过引入新概念'surfaces'实现了线性时间复杂度的构建。
AI.motivation: 传统回文树无法高效处理动态字符串的双端队列操作及最长回文前后缀查询，需设计新型数据结构以满足在线操作需求。
AI.method: 通过识别并维护一种新型子串出现形式'surfaces'（既不是其他回文子串的前缀也不是后缀的回文出现），建立双端eertree的线性时间维护机制。
AI.result: 成功构建了支持双端操作的eertree，可在线性时间内完成回文子串计数、最长回文前后缀查询等任务，并证明surfaces概念的独立研究价值。
AI.conclusion: 双端eertree通过surfaces机制实现了动态回文处理的线性时间复杂度，为回文子串研究提供了新工具和理论突破。

authors_text: Zhengcong Fei,Mingyuan Fan,Li Zhu,Junshi Huang
title: Progressive Text-to-Image Generation
title_zh: 渐进式文本到图像生成
comment: Technique report
comments: Technique report
abstract: Recently, Vector Quantized AutoRegressive (VQ-AR) models have shown remarkable results in text-to-image synthesis by equally predicting discrete image tokens from the top left to bottom right in the latent space. Although the simple generative process surprisingly works well, is this the best way to generate the image? For instance, human creation is more inclined to the outline-to-fine of an image, while VQ-AR models themselves do not consider any relative importance of image patches. In this paper, we present a progressive model for high-fidelity text-to-image generation. The proposed method takes effect by creating new image tokens from coarse to fine based on the existing context in a parallel manner, and this procedure is recursively applied with the proposed error revision mechanism until an image sequence is completed. The resulting coarse-to-fine hierarchy makes the image generation process intuitive and interpretable. Extensive experiments in MS COCO benchmark demonstrate that the progressive model produces significantly better results compared with the previous VQ-AR method in FID score across a wide variety of categories and aspects. Moreover, the design of parallel generation in each step allows more than $\times 13$ inference acceleration with slight performance loss.
summary: Recently, Vector Quantized AutoRegressive (VQ-AR) models have shown remarkable results in text-to-image synthesis by equally predicting discrete image tokens from the top left to bottom right in the latent space. Although the simple generative process surprisingly works well, is this the best way to generate the image? For instance, human creation is more inclined to the outline-to-fine of an image, while VQ-AR models themselves do not consider any relative importance of image patches. In this paper, we present a progressive model for high-fidelity text-to-image generation. The proposed method takes effect by creating new image tokens from coarse to fine based on the existing context in a parallel manner, and this procedure is recursively applied with the proposed error revision mechanism until an image sequence is completed. The resulting coarse-to-fine hierarchy makes the image generation process intuitive and interpretable. Extensive experiments in MS COCO benchmark demonstrate that the progressive model produces significantly better results compared with the previous VQ-AR method in FID score across a wide variety of categories and aspects. Moreover, the design of parallel generation in each step allows more than $\times 13$ inference acceleration with slight performance loss.
abstract_zh: 最近，矢量量化自回归（VQ-AR）模型通过同样预测潜在空间中从左上到右下的离散图像标记，在文本到图像合成方面显示出了显着的结果。尽管简单的生成过程出奇地有效，但这是否是生成图像的最佳方式？例如，人类创作更倾向于图像的轮廓到精细，而 VQ-AR 模型本身并不考虑图像块的任何相对重要性。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于高保真文本到图像生成的渐进模型。所提出的方法通过基于现有上下文以并行方式从粗到细地创建新的图像标记来生效，并且该过程递归地应用所提出的错误修正机制，直到完成图像序列。由此产生的从粗到细的层次结构使图像生成过程直观且可解释。 MS COCO 基准测试中的大量实验表明，与之前的 VQ-AR 方法相比，渐进模型在各种类别和方面的 FID 评分方面产生了明显更好的结果。此外，每个步骤中并行生成的设计允许超过 $\times 13$ 的推理加速，而性能损失却很小。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种渐进式文本到图像生成模型，通过从粗到细的并行生成方式改进现有VQ-AR方法。
AI.motivation: 现有VQ-AR模型按固定顺序生成图像令牌，未考虑图像区域的相对重要性，而人类创作更倾向于从轮廓到细节的渐进过程。
AI.method: 提出基于现有上下文的并行粗到细令牌生成方法，结合递归误差修正机制，构建可解释的层次化生成过程。
AI.result: 在MS COCO基准测试中，FID分数显著优于VQ-AR方法，且推理速度提升13倍以上。
AI.conclusion: 渐进式生成方法在图像质量与生成效率之间取得了更好平衡，为文本到图像合成提供了新思路。

authors_text: Luyuan Xie,Yan Zhong,Lin Yang,Zhaoyu Yan,Zhonghai Wu,Junjie Wang
title: TC-SKNet with GridMask for Low-complexity Classification of Acoustic scene
title_zh: 带有 GridMask 的 TC-SKNet 用于声学场景的低复杂度分类
comment: Accepted to APSIPA ASC 2022
comments: Accepted to APSIPA ASC 2022
abstract: Convolution neural networks (CNNs) have good performance in low-complexity classification tasks such as acoustic scene classifications (ASCs). However, there are few studies on the relationship between the length of target speech and the size of the convolution kernels. In this paper, we combine Selective Kernel Network with Temporal-Convolution (TC-SKNet) to adjust the receptive field of convolution kernels to solve the problem of variable length of target voice while keeping low-complexity. GridMask is a data augmentation strategy by masking part of the raw data or feature area. It can enhance the generalization of the model as the role of dropout. In our experiments, the performance gain brought by GridMask is stronger than spectrum augmentation in ASCs. Finally, we adopt AutoML to search best structure of TC-SKNet and hyperparameters of GridMask for improving the classification performance. As a result, a peak accuracy of 59.87% TC-SKNet is equivalent to that of SOTA, but the parameters only use 20.9 K.
summary: Convolution neural networks (CNNs) have good performance in low-complexity classification tasks such as acoustic scene classifications (ASCs). However, there are few studies on the relationship between the length of target speech and the size of the convolution kernels. In this paper, we combine Selective Kernel Network with Temporal-Convolution (TC-SKNet) to adjust the receptive field of convolution kernels to solve the problem of variable length of target voice while keeping low-complexity. GridMask is a data augmentation strategy by masking part of the raw data or feature area. It can enhance the generalization of the model as the role of dropout. In our experiments, the performance gain brought by GridMask is stronger than spectrum augmentation in ASCs. Finally, we adopt AutoML to search best structure of TC-SKNet and hyperparameters of GridMask for improving the classification performance. As a result, a peak accuracy of 59.87% TC-SKNet is equivalent to that of SOTA, but the parameters only use 20.9 K.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）在声学场景分类（ASC）等低复杂度分类任务中具有良好的性能。然而，关于目标语音长度与卷积核大小之间关系的研究很少。在本文中，我们将选择性核网络与时间卷积（TC-SKNet）相结合，调整卷积核的感受野，以解决目标语音长度可变的问题，同时保持低复杂度。 GridMask是一种通过屏蔽部分原始数据或特征区域的数据增强策略。作为dropout的作用可以增强模型的泛化能力。在我们的实验中，GridMask 带来的性能增益比 ASC 中的频谱增强更强。最后，我们采用AutoML搜索TC-SKNet的最佳结构和GridMask的超参数以提高分类性能。结果，TC-SKNet 的峰值精度为 59.87%，与 SOTA 相当，但参数仅使用 20.9 K。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合TC-SKNet与GridMask的低复杂度声学场景分类方法。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对目标语音长度与卷积核尺寸关系的探索，需在保持低复杂度的同时解决语音长度可变问题。
AI.method: 融合可调接收域的TC-SKNet网络与GridMask数据增强策略，并通过AutoML优化网络结构与超参数。
AI.result: 在保持20.9K参数量下达到59.87%分类准确率，性能与SOTA方法相当。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在低参数量下实现了声学场景分类的最优性能，验证了动态卷积结构与GridMask的有效性。

authors_text: Nathaniel Braman,Prateek Prasanna,Kaustav Bera,Mehdi Alilou,Mohammadhadi Khorrami,Patrick Leo,Maryam Etesami,Manasa Vulchi,Paulette Turk,Amit Gupta,Prantesh Jain,Pingfu Fu,Nathan Pennell,Vamsidhar Velcheti,Jame Abraham,Donna Plecha,Anant Madabhushi
title: Novel Radiomic Measurements of Tumor- Associated Vasculature Morphology on Clinical Imaging as a Biomarker of Treatment Response in Multiple Cancers
title_zh: 临床成像上肿瘤相关脉管系统形态学的新型放射组学测量作为多种癌症治疗反应的生物标志物
comment: This manuscript has been accepted for publication in Clinical Cancer Research, which is published by the American Association for Cancer Research
comments: This manuscript has been accepted for publication in Clinical Cancer Research, which is published by the American Association for Cancer Research
abstract: Purpose: Tumor-associated vasculature differs from healthy blood vessels by its chaotic architecture and twistedness, which promotes treatment resistance. Measurable differences in these attributes may help stratify patients by likely benefit of systemic therapy (e.g. chemotherapy). In this work, we present a new category of radiomic biomarkers called quantitative tumor-associated vasculature (QuanTAV) features, and demonstrate their ability to predict response and survival across multiple cancers, imaging modalities, and treatment regimens. Experimental Design: We segmented tumor vessels and computed mathematical measurements of twistedness and organization on routine pre-treatment radiology (CT or contrast-enhanced MRI) from 558 patients, who received one of four first-line chemotherapy-based therapeutic intervention strategies for breast (n=371) or non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC, n=187). Results: Across 4 chemotherapy-based treatment strategies, classifiers of QuanTAV measurements significantly (p<.05) predicted response in held out testing cohorts alone (AUC=0.63-0.71) and increased AUC by 0.06-0.12 when added to models of significant clinical variables alone. QuanTAV risk scores were prognostic of recurrence free survival in treatment cohorts chemotherapy for breast cancer (p=0.002, HR=1.25, 95% CI 1.08-1.44, C-index=.66) and chemoradiation for NSCLC (p=0.039, HR=1.28, 95% CI 1.01-1.62, C-index=0.66). Categorical QuanTAV risk groups were independently prognostic among all treatment groups, including NSCLC patients receiving chemotherapy (p=0.034, HR=2.29, 95% CI 1.07-4.94, C-index=0.62). Conclusions: Across these domains, we observed an association of vascular morphology on radiology with treatment outcome. Our findings suggest the potential of tumor-associated vasculature shape and structure as a prognostic and predictive biomarker for multiple cancers and treatments.

In this work, we present a new category of radiomic biomarkers called quantitative tumor-associated vasculature (QuanTAV) features, and demonstrate their ability to predict response and survival across multiple cancers, imaging modalities, and treatment regimens. QuanTAV risk scores were prognostic of recurrence free survival in treatment cohorts chemotherapy for breast cancer (p=0.002, HR=1.25, 95% CI 1.08-1.44, C-index=.66) and chemoradiation for NSCLC (p=0.039, HR=1.28, 95% CI 1.01-1.62, C-index=0.66). Categorical QuanTAV risk groups were independently prognostic among all treatment groups, including NSCLC patients receiving chemotherapy (p=0.034, HR=2.29, 95% CI 1.07-4.94, C-index=0.62). abstract_zh: 目的：肿瘤相关脉管系统与健康血管的不同之处在于其混乱的结构和扭曲性，这会增加治疗抵抗力。这些属性的可测量差异可能有助于根据全身治疗（例如化疗）的可能益处对患者进行分层。在这项工作中，我们提出了一类新的放射组学生物标志物，称为定量肿瘤相关脉管系统（QuanTAV）特征，并证明了它们预测多种癌症、成像方式和治疗方案的反应和生存的能力。实验设计：我们对 558 名患者进行常规治疗前放射学检查（CT 或对比增强 MRI），对肿瘤血管进行了分割，并计算了扭曲和组织的数学测量结果，这些患者接受了乳腺癌 (n = 371) 或非小细胞肺癌 (NSCLC，n = 187) 四种一线化疗治疗干预策略中的一种。结果：在 4 种基于化疗的治疗策略中，QuanTAV 测量值的分类器显着 (p<.05) 预测单独测试队列中的反应 (AUC=0.63-0.71)，并且当单独添加到显着临床变量模型时，AUC 增加 0.06-0.12。 QuanTAV 风险评分是乳腺癌化疗（p=0.002，HR=1.25，95% CI 1.08-1.44，C-index=.66）和 NSCLC 放化疗（p=0.039，HR=1.28，95% CI 1.01-1.62，C-index=0.66）治疗队列中无复发生存的预后。 QuanTAV 分类风险组在所有治疗组中具有独立的预后，包括接受化疗的 NSCLC 患者（p=0.034，HR=2.29，95% CI 1.07-4.94，C 指数=0.62）。结论：在这些领域中，我们观察到放射学上的血管形态与治疗结果之间的关联。我们的研究结果表明肿瘤相关脉管系统形状和结构作为多种癌症和治疗的预后和预测生物标志物的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新型放射组学生物标志物QuanTAV，通过量化肿瘤相关血管形态特征，预测多种癌症患者对化疗等治疗的反应及生存率。
AI.motivation: 肿瘤相关血管结构异常（如扭曲、无序）会导致治疗抵抗，但现有方法难以有效量化这些特征以指导治疗决策，需开发新型生物标志物。
AI.method: 对558例癌症患者术前影像（CT/MRI）进行肿瘤血管分割，提取数学特征（扭曲度、组织性）并构建QuanTAV模型，结合临床变量验证其预测效能。
AI.result: QuanTAV特征可独立预测化疗反应（AUC 0.63-0.71），联合临床模型后AUC提升0.06-0.12；风险评分显著关联乳腺癌和NSCLC的无复发生存率（HR 1.25-1.28）。
AI.conclusion: 肿瘤血管形态特征与治疗结局相关，QuanTAV具有作为多癌种治疗预测及预后评估生物标志物的潜力。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种新型放射组学生物标志物QuanTAV，通过量化肿瘤相关血管形态特征，预测多种癌症患者对化疗等治疗的反应及生存率。
ai_content.motivation: 肿瘤相关血管结构异常（如扭曲、无序）会导致治疗抵抗，但现有方法难以有效量化这些特征以指导治疗决策，需开发新型生物标志物。
ai_content.method: 对558例癌症患者术前影像（CT/MRI）进行肿瘤血管分割，提取数学特征（扭曲度、组织性）并构建QuanTAV模型，结合临床变量验证其预测效能。
ai_content.result: QuanTAV特征可独立预测化疗反应（AUC 0.63-0.71），联合临床模型后AUC提升0.06-0.12；风险评分显著关联乳腺癌和NSCLC的无复发生存率（HR 1.25-1.28）。
ai_content.conclusion: 肿瘤血管形态特征与治疗结局相关，QuanTAV具有作为多癌种治疗预测及预后评估生物标志物的潜力。

search_text: Novel Radiomic Measurements of Tumor- Associated Vasculature Morphology on Clinical Imaging as a Biomarker of Treatment Response in Multiple Cancers 临床成像上肿瘤相关脉管系统形态学的新型放射组学测量作为多种癌症治疗反应的生物标志物 Purpose: Tumor-associated vasculature differs from healthy blood vessels by its chaotic architecture and twistedness, which promotes treatment resistance. Measurable differences in these attributes may help stratify patients by likely benefit of systemic therapy (e.g. chemotherapy). In this work, we present a new category of radiomic biomarkers called quantitative tumor-associated vasculature (QuanTAV) features, and demonstrate their ability to predict response and survival across multiple cancers, imaging modalities, and treatment regimens. Experimental Design: We segmented tumor vessels and computed mathematical measurements of twistedness and organization on routine pre-treatment radiology (CT or contrast-enhanced MRI) from 558 patients, who received one of four first-line chemotherapy-based therapeutic intervention strategies for breast (n=371) or non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC, n=187). Results: Across 4 chemotherapy-based treatment strategies, classifiers of QuanTAV measurements significantly (p<.05) predicted response in held out testing cohorts alone (AUC=0.63-0.71) and increased AUC by 0.06-0.12 when added to models of significant clinical variables alone. QuanTAV risk scores were prognostic of recurrence free survival in treatment cohorts chemotherapy for breast cancer (p=0.002, HR=1.25, 95% CI 1.08-1.44, C-index=.66) and chemoradiation for NSCLC (p=0.039, HR=1.28, 95% CI 1.01-1.62, C-index=0.66). Categorical QuanTAV risk groups were independently prognostic among all treatment groups, including NSCLC patients receiving chemotherapy (p=0.034, HR=2.29, 95% CI 1.07-4.94, C-index=0.62). Conclusions: Across these domains, we observed an association of vascular morphology on radiology with treatment outcome. Our findings suggest the potential of tumor-associated vasculature shape and structure as a prognostic and predictive biomarker for multiple cancers and treatments. 目的：肿瘤相关脉管系统与健康血管的不同之处在于其混乱的结构和扭曲性，这会增加治疗抵抗力。这些属性的可测量差异可能有助于根据全身治疗（例如化疗）的可能益处对患者进行分层。在这项工作中，我们提出了一类新的放射组学生物标志物，称为定量肿瘤相关脉管系统（QuanTAV）特征，并证明了它们预测多种癌症、成像方式和治疗方案的反应和生存的能力。实验设计：我们对 558 名患者进行常规治疗前放射学检查（CT 或对比增强 MRI），对肿瘤血管进行了分割，并计算了扭曲和组织的数学测量结果，这些患者接受了乳腺癌 (n = 371) 或非小细胞肺癌 (NSCLC，n = 187) 四种一线化疗治疗干预策略中的一种。结果：在 4 种基于化疗的治疗策略中，QuanTAV 测量值的分类器显着 (p<.05) 预测单独测试队列中的反应 (AUC=0.63-0.71)，并且当单独添加到显着临床变量模型时，AUC 增加 0.06-0.12。 QuanTAV 风险评分是乳腺癌化疗（p=0.002，HR=1.25，95% CI 1.08-1.44，C-index=.66）和 NSCLC 放化疗（p=0.039，HR=1.28，95% CI 1.01-1.62，C-index=0.66）治疗队列中无复发生存的预后。 QuanTAV 分类风险组在所有治疗组中具有独立的预后，包括接受化疗的 NSCLC 患者（p=0.034，HR=2.29，95% CI 1.07-4.94，C 指数=0.62）。结论：在这些领域中，我们观察到放射学上的血管形态与治疗结果之间的关联。我们的研究结果表明肿瘤相关脉管系统形状和结构作为多种癌症和治疗的预后和预测生物标志物的潜力。 本文提出一种新型放射组学生物标志物QuanTAV，通过量化肿瘤相关血管形态特征，预测多种癌症患者对化疗等治疗的反应及生存率。 Nathaniel Braman,Prateek Prasanna,Kaustav Bera,Mehdi Alilou,Mohammadhadi Khorrami,Patrick Leo,Maryam Etesami,Manasa Vulchi,Paulette Turk,Amit Gupta,Prantesh Jain,Pingfu Fu,Nathan Pennell,Vamsidhar Velcheti,Jame Abraham,Donna Plecha,Anant Madabhushi q-bio.QM,cs.CV,q-bio.TO

authors_text: Junjie Chen,Li Niu,Siyuan Zhou,Jianlou Si,Chen Qian,Liqing Zhang
title: Weak-shot Semantic Segmentation via Dual Similarity Transfer
title_zh: 通过双重相似性迁移进行弱样本语义分割
comment: accepted by NeurIPS2022
comments: accepted by NeurIPS2022
abstract: Semantic segmentation is an important and prevalent task, but severely suffers from the high cost of pixel-level annotations when extending to more classes in wider applications. To this end, we focus on the problem named weak-shot semantic segmentation, where the novel classes are learnt from cheaper image-level labels with the support of base classes having off-the-shelf pixel-level labels. To tackle this problem, we propose SimFormer, which performs dual similarity transfer upon MaskFormer. Specifically, MaskFormer disentangles the semantic segmentation task into two sub-tasks: proposal classification and proposal segmentation for each proposal. Proposal segmentation allows proposal-pixel similarity transfer from base classes to novel classes, which enables the mask learning of novel classes. We also learn pixel-pixel similarity from base classes and distill such class-agnostic semantic similarity to the semantic masks of novel classes, which regularizes the segmentation model with pixel-level semantic relationship across images. In addition, we propose a complementary loss to facilitate the learning of novel classes. Comprehensive experiments on the challenging COCO-Stuff-10K and ADE20K datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. Codes are available at https://github.com/bcmi/SimFormer-Weak-Shot-Semantic-Segmentation.
summary: Semantic segmentation is an important and prevalent task, but severely suffers from the high cost of pixel-level annotations when extending to more classes in wider applications. To this end, we focus on the problem named weak-shot semantic segmentation, where the novel classes are learnt from cheaper image-level labels with the support of base classes having off-the-shelf pixel-level labels. To tackle this problem, we propose SimFormer, which performs dual similarity transfer upon MaskFormer. Specifically, MaskFormer disentangles the semantic segmentation task into two sub-tasks: proposal classification and proposal segmentation for each proposal. Proposal segmentation allows proposal-pixel similarity transfer from base classes to novel classes, which enables the mask learning of novel classes. We also learn pixel-pixel similarity from base classes and distill such class-agnostic semantic similarity to the semantic masks of novel classes, which regularizes the segmentation model with pixel-level semantic relationship across images. In addition, we propose a complementary loss to facilitate the learning of novel classes. Comprehensive experiments on the challenging COCO-Stuff-10K and ADE20K datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. Codes are available at https://github.com/bcmi/SimFormer-Weak-Shot-Semantic-Segmentation.
abstract_zh: 语义分割是一项重要且普遍的任务，但在扩展到更广泛的应用中的更多类别时，严重受到像素级注释的高成本的影响。为此，我们关注弱镜头语义分割问题，其中新类是在具有现成像素级标签的基类的支持下从更便宜的图像级标签中学习的。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SimFormer，它在 MaskFormer 上执行双重相似性转移。具体来说，MaskFormer 将语义分割任务分解为两个子任务：提案分类和每个提案的提案分割。提案分割允许提案像素相似性从基类转移到新类，从而实现新类的掩模学习。我们还从基类中学习像素到像素的相似性，并将这种与类无关的语义相似性提炼为新类的语义掩码，从而通过图像之间的像素级语义关系规范分割模型。此外，我们提出了补充损失以促进新课程的学习。对具有挑战性的 COCO-Stuff-10K 和 ADE20K 数据集的综合实验证明了我们方法的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/bcmi/SimFormer-Weak-Shot-Semantic-Segmentation 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种弱标注语义分割方法SimFormer，通过双重相似性迁移解决新类别学习问题。
AI.motivation: 传统语义分割依赖昂贵的像素级标注，而扩展到更多类别时成本过高。弱标注语义分割通过图像级标签学习新类别，降低标注成本。
AI.method: 基于MaskFormer框架，SimFormer通过提案分割实现提案-像素相似性迁移，并学习像素-像素相似性进行知识蒸馏，同时引入互补损失优化新类别学习。
AI.result: 在COCO-Stuff-10K和ADE20K数据集上的实验验证了方法有效性，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 双重相似性迁移策略有效提升了弱标注场景下语义分割性能，为新类别学习提供了高效解决方案。

To this end, we focus on the problem named weak-shot semantic segmentation, where the novel classes are learnt from cheaper image-level labels with the support of base classes having off-the-shelf pixel-level labels. We also learn pixel-pixel similarity from base classes and distill such class-agnostic semantic similarity to the semantic masks of novel classes, which regularizes the segmentation model with pixel-level semantic relationship across images. In addition, we propose a complementary loss to facilitate the learning of novel classes. Comprehensive experiments on the challenging COCO-Stuff-10K and ADE20K datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. authors_text: Mohammad Divband Soorati,Enrico H. Gerding,Enrico Marchioni,Pavel Naumov,Timothy J. Norman,Sarvapali D. Ramchurn,Bahar Rastegari,Adam Sobey,Sebastian Stein,Danesh Tarpore,Vahid Yazdanpanah,Jie Zhang
title: From Intelligent Agents to Trustworthy Human-Centred Multiagent Systems
title_zh: 从智能代理到值得信赖的以人为中心的多代理系统
comment: Appears in the Special Issue on Multi-Agent Systems Research in the United Kingdom
comments: Appears in the Special Issue on Multi-Agent Systems Research in the United Kingdom
abstract: The Agents, Interaction and Complexity research group at the University of Southampton has a long track record of research in multiagent systems (MAS). We have made substantial scientific contributions across learning in MAS, game-theoretic techniques for coordinating agent systems, and formal methods for representation and reasoning. We highlight key results achieved by the group and elaborate on recent work and open research challenges in developing trustworthy autonomous systems and deploying human-centred AI systems that aim to support societal good.
summary: The Agents, Interaction and Complexity research group at the University of Southampton has a long track record of research in multiagent systems (MAS). We have made substantial scientific contributions across learning in MAS, game-theoretic techniques for coordinating agent systems, and formal methods for representation and reasoning. We highlight key results achieved by the group and elaborate on recent work and open research challenges in developing trustworthy autonomous systems and deploying human-centred AI systems that aim to support societal good.
abstract_zh: 南安普顿大学的代理、交互和复杂性研究小组在多代理系统 (MAS) 方面拥有长期的研究记录。我们在 MAS 学习、协调代理系统的博弈论技术以及表示和推理的形式化方法方面做出了重大的科学贡献。我们重点介绍了该小组取得的主要成果，并详细阐述了在开发值得信赖的自主系统和部署旨在支持社会公益的以人为本的人工智能系统方面的最新工作和开放研究挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文概述了南安普顿大学研究团队在多智能体系统（MAS）领域的长期研究，重点包括学习、博弈论协调技术及形式化方法，并探讨可信自主系统和以人为本AI系统的挑战。
AI.motivation: 推动可信且以人类为中心的自主系统发展，以解决复杂社会问题并促进技术对社会的积极影响。
AI.method: 结合机器学习、博弈论协调机制及形式化方法，用于智能体系统的建模、推理与可信度保障。
AI.result: 展示了团队在MAS领域的关键成果，明确了可信自主系统与人类中心AI的前沿挑战与研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 可信与以人为本的多智能体系统是未来AI发展的核心，需跨学科合作解决技术与伦理难题。

motivation: 推动可信且以人类为中心的自主系统发展，以解决复杂社会问题并促进技术对社会的积极影响。
method: 结合机器学习、博弈论协调机制及形式化方法，用于智能体系统的建模、推理与可信度保障。
result: 展示了团队在MAS领域的关键成果，明确了可信自主系统与人类中心AI的前沿挑战与研究方向。
conclusion: 可信与以人为本的多智能体系统是未来AI发展的核心，需跨学科合作解决技术与伦理难题。
search_text: From Intelligent Agents to Trustworthy Human-Centred Multiagent Systems 从智能代理到值得信赖的以人为中心的多代理系统 The Agents, Interaction and Complexity research group at the University of Southampton has a long track record of research in multiagent systems (MAS). We have made substantial scientific contributions across learning in MAS, game-theoretic techniques for coordinating agent systems, and formal methods for representation and reasoning. We highlight key results achieved by the group and elaborate on recent work and open research challenges in developing trustworthy autonomous systems and deploying human-centred AI systems that aim to support societal good. 南安普顿大学的代理、交互和复杂性研究小组在多代理系统 (MAS) 方面拥有长期的研究记录。我们在 MAS 学习、协调代理系统的博弈论技术以及表示和推理的形式化方法方面做出了重大的科学贡献。我们重点介绍了该小组取得的主要成果，并详细阐述了在开发值得信赖的自主系统和部署旨在支持社会公益的以人为本的人工智能系统方面的最新工作和开放研究挑战。 本文概述了南安普顿大学研究团队在多智能体系统（MAS）领域的长期研究，重点包括学习、博弈论协调技术及形式化方法，并探讨可信自主系统和以人为本AI系统的挑战。 Mohammad Divband Soorati,Enrico H. Gerding,Enrico Marchioni,Pavel Naumov,Timothy J. Norman,Sarvapali D. Ramchurn,Bahar Rastegari,Adam Sobey,Sebastian Stein,Danesh Tarpore,Vahid Yazdanpanah,Jie Zhang cs.MA,cs.AI

authors_text: Arundhati Banerjee,Ramina Ghods,Jeff Schneider
title: Cost Aware Asynchronous Multi-Agent Active Search
title_zh: 成本感知异步多代理主动搜索
abstract: Multi-agent active search requires autonomous agents to choose sensing actions that efficiently locate targets. In a realistic setting, agents also must consider the costs that their decisions incur. Previously proposed active search algorithms simplify the problem by ignoring uncertainty in the agent's environment, using myopic decision making, and/or overlooking costs. In this paper, we introduce an online active search algorithm to detect targets in an unknown environment by making adaptive cost-aware decisions regarding the agent's actions. Our algorithm combines principles from Thompson Sampling (for search space exploration and decentralized multi-agent decision making), Monte Carlo Tree Search (for long horizon planning) and pareto-optimal confidence bounds (for multi-objective optimization in an unknown environment) to propose an online lookahead planner that removes all the simplifications. We analyze the algorithm's performance in simulation to show its efficacy in cost aware active search.
summary: Multi-agent active search requires autonomous agents to choose sensing actions that efficiently locate targets. In a realistic setting, agents also must consider the costs that their decisions incur. Previously proposed active search algorithms simplify the problem by ignoring uncertainty in the agent's environment, using myopic decision making, and/or overlooking costs. In this paper, we introduce an online active search algorithm to detect targets in an unknown environment by making adaptive cost-aware decisions regarding the agent's actions. Our algorithm combines principles from Thompson Sampling (for search space exploration and decentralized multi-agent decision making), Monte Carlo Tree Search (for long horizon planning) and pareto-optimal confidence bounds (for multi-objective optimization in an unknown environment) to propose an online lookahead planner that removes all the simplifications. We analyze the algorithm's performance in simulation to show its efficacy in cost aware active search.
abstract_zh: 多智能体主动搜索需要自主智能体选择有效定位目标的感知动作。在现实环境中，代理人还必须考虑其决策所产生的成本。先前提出的主动搜索算法通过忽略代理环境中的不确定性、使用短视决策和/或忽略成本来简化问题。在本文中，我们引入了一种在线主动搜索算法，通过对代理的行为做出自适应成本感知决策来检测未知环境中的目标。我们的算法结合了汤普森采样（用于搜索空间探索和去中心化多智能体决策）、蒙特卡罗树搜索（用于长期规划）和帕累托最优置信界限（用于未知环境中的多目标优化）的原理，提出了一种消除所有简化的在线前瞻规划器。我们分析了该算法在仿真中的性能，以展示其在成本感知主动搜索中的功效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种考虑成本的异步多智能体主动搜索算法，解决现实环境中因忽略不确定性和成本导致的效率问题。
AI.motivation: 现有主动搜索算法通过简化问题（如忽略环境不确定性、采用近视决策或忽视成本）导致实际应用效果受限，需更全面的解决方案。
AI.method: 融合汤普森采样（探索与分布式决策）、蒙特卡洛树搜索（长期规划）及帕累托最优置信界（多目标优化），设计在线前瞻性规划器。
AI.result: 仿真结果验证了该算法在成本感知主动搜索任务中的有效性，能够适应未知环境的多目标优化需求。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法通过综合多种技术消除了传统方法的简化假设，在复杂环境中实现了更优的成本感知主动搜索性能。

authors_text: Haoyu Chen,Linqi Song,Zhenxing Qian,Xinpeng Zhang,Kede Ma
title: Hiding Images in Deep Probabilistic Models
title_zh: 在深度概率模型中隐藏图像
abstract: Data hiding with deep neural networks (DNNs) has experienced impressive successes in recent years. A prevailing scheme is to train an autoencoder, consisting of an encoding network to embed (or transform) secret messages in (or into) a carrier, and a decoding network to extract the hidden messages. This scheme may suffer from several limitations regarding practicability, security, and embedding capacity. In this work, we describe a different computational framework to hide images in deep probabilistic models. Specifically, we use a DNN to model the probability density of cover images, and hide a secret image in one particular location of the learned distribution. As an instantiation, we adopt a SinGAN, a pyramid of generative adversarial networks (GANs), to learn the patch distribution of one cover image. We hide the secret image by fitting a deterministic mapping from a fixed set of noise maps (generated by an embedding key) to the secret image during patch distribution learning. The stego SinGAN, behaving as the original SinGAN, is publicly communicated; only the receiver with the embedding key is able to extract the secret image. We demonstrate the feasibility of our SinGAN approach in terms of extraction accuracy and model security. Moreover, we show the flexibility of the proposed method in terms of hiding multiple images for different receivers and obfuscating the secret image.
summary: Data hiding with deep neural networks (DNNs) has experienced impressive successes in recent years. A prevailing scheme is to train an autoencoder, consisting of an encoding network to embed (or transform) secret messages in (or into) a carrier, and a decoding network to extract the hidden messages. This scheme may suffer from several limitations regarding practicability, security, and embedding capacity. In this work, we describe a different computational framework to hide images in deep probabilistic models. Specifically, we use a DNN to model the probability density of cover images, and hide a secret image in one particular location of the learned distribution. As an instantiation, we adopt a SinGAN, a pyramid of generative adversarial networks (GANs), to learn the patch distribution of one cover image. We hide the secret image by fitting a deterministic mapping from a fixed set of noise maps (generated by an embedding key) to the secret image during patch distribution learning. The stego SinGAN, behaving as the original SinGAN, is publicly communicated; only the receiver with the embedding key is able to extract the secret image. We demonstrate the feasibility of our SinGAN approach in terms of extraction accuracy and model security. Moreover, we show the flexibility of the proposed method in terms of hiding multiple images for different receivers and obfuscating the secret image.
abstract_zh: 近年来，利用深度神经网络 (DNN) 进行数据隐藏取得了令人瞩目的成功。流行的方案是训练自动编码器，由用于将秘密消息嵌入（或转换）到载体中的编码网络和用于提取隐藏消息的解码网络组成。该方案可能会受到实用性、安全性和嵌入容量方面的一些限制。在这项工作中，我们描述了一种不同的计算框架来将图像隐藏在深度概率模型中。具体来说，我们使用 DNN 对覆盖图像的概率密度进行建模，并将秘密图像隐藏在学习分布的一个特定位置。作为实例，我们采用 SinGAN（生成对抗网络（GAN）的金字塔）来学习一张封面图像的补丁分布。我们通过在补丁分布学习期间将一组固定的噪声图（由嵌入密钥生成）的确定性映射拟合到秘密图像来隐藏秘密图像。 Stego SinGAN 与原始 SinGAN 一样，是公开传播的；只有拥有嵌入密钥的接收者才能提取秘密图像。我们证明了 SinGAN 方法在提取精度和模型安全性方面的可行性。此外，我们展示了所提出的方法在隐藏不同接收者的多个图像和混淆秘密图像方面的灵活性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度概率模型的图像隐藏方法，通过SinGAN框架在覆盖图像中嵌入秘密图像。
AI.motivation: 现有基于自编码器的图像隐藏方法存在实用性、安全性和嵌入容量的局限，需探索更优的隐藏框架。
AI.method: 利用SinGAN学习覆盖图像的补丁分布，通过嵌入密钥生成的噪声图与秘密图像建立确定性映射实现隐藏。
AI.result: 验证了该方法在提取准确率和模型安全性上的可行性，并展示了多图像隐藏与混淆能力。
AI.conclusion: SinGAN框架为图像隐藏提供了新思路，在安全性与灵活性方面优于传统方法。

authors_text: Jon Almazán,Byungsoo Ko,Geonmo Gu,Diane Larlus,Yannis Kalantidis
title: Granularity-aware Adaptation for Image Retrieval over Multiple Tasks
title_zh: 多任务图像检索的粒度感知适应
comment: ECCV 2022
comments: ECCV 2022
abstract: Strong image search models can be learned for a specific domain, ie. set of labels, provided that some labeled images of that domain are available. A practical visual search model, however, should be versatile enough to solve multiple retrieval tasks simultaneously, even if those cover very different specialized domains. Additionally, it should be able to benefit from even unlabeled images from these various retrieval tasks. This is the more practical scenario that we consider in this paper. We address it with the proposed Grappa, an approach that starts from a strong pretrained model, and adapts it to tackle multiple retrieval tasks concurrently, using only unlabeled images from the different task domains. We extend the pretrained model with multiple independently trained sets of adaptors that use pseudo-label sets of different sizes, effectively mimicking different pseudo-granularities. We reconcile all adaptor sets into a single unified model suited for all retrieval tasks by learning fusion layers that we guide by propagating pseudo-granularity attentions across neighbors in the feature space. Results on a benchmark composed of six heterogeneous retrieval tasks show that the unsupervised Grappa model improves the zero-shot performance of a state-of-the-art self-supervised learning model, and in some places reaches or improves over a task label-aware oracle that selects the most fitting pseudo-granularity per task.
summary: Strong image search models can be learned for a specific domain, ie. set of labels, provided that some labeled images of that domain are available. A practical visual search model, however, should be versatile enough to solve multiple retrieval tasks simultaneously, even if those cover very different specialized domains. Additionally, it should be able to benefit from even unlabeled images from these various retrieval tasks. This is the more practical scenario that we consider in this paper. We address it with the proposed Grappa, an approach that starts from a strong pretrained model, and adapts it to tackle multiple retrieval tasks concurrently, using only unlabeled images from the different task domains. We extend the pretrained model with multiple independently trained sets of adaptors that use pseudo-label sets of different sizes, effectively mimicking different pseudo-granularities. We reconcile all adaptor sets into a single unified model suited for all retrieval tasks by learning fusion layers that we guide by propagating pseudo-granularity attentions across neighbors in the feature space. Results on a benchmark composed of six heterogeneous retrieval tasks show that the unsupervised Grappa model improves the zero-shot performance of a state-of-the-art self-supervised learning model, and in some places reaches or improves over a task label-aware oracle that selects the most fitting pseudo-granularity per task.
abstract_zh: 可以针对特定领域学习强大的图像搜索模型，即。一组标签，前提是该域的一些标记图像可用。然而，实用的视觉搜索模型应该足够通用，能够同时解决多个检索任务，即使这些任务涵盖非常不同的专业领域。此外，它应该能够从这些不同的检索任务中甚至未标记的图像中受益。这是我们在本文中考虑的更实际的场景。我们使用提出的 Grappa 来解决这个问题，这是一种从强大的预训练模型开始的方法，并对其进行调整以同时处理多个检索任务，仅使用来自不同任务域的未标记图像。我们使用多个独立训练的适配器组扩展了预训练模型，这些适配器组使用不同大小的伪标签集，有效地模仿不同的伪粒度。我们通过学习融合层，将所有适配器集协调成一个适合所有检索任务的统一模型，我们通过在特征空间中的邻居之间传播伪粒度注意力来引导融合层。由六个异构检索任务组成的基准测试结果表明，无监督 Grappa 模型提高了最先进的自监督学习模型的零样本性能，并且在某些地方达到或改进了任务标签感知预言机，该预言机为每个任务选择最合适的伪粒度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Grappa的方法，通过使用未标记图像和伪粒度注意力机制，使预训练模型能够同时适应多种异构图像检索任务。
AI.motivation: 现有图像检索模型通常针对特定领域且依赖标注数据，难以处理多任务场景。实际需求要求模型能跨不同领域同时处理多个任务，并有效利用未标记数据。
AI.method: 基于预训练模型，扩展多个独立适配器处理不同粒度伪标签，通过特征空间中邻居间的伪粒度注意力传播，学习融合层实现多任务统一。
AI.result: 在包含六个异构任务的基准测试中，无监督的Grappa模型在零样本场景下超越了最先进的自监督学习模型，部分任务表现接近或优于基于任务标签的最优选择器。
AI.conclusion: 粒度感知适配方法有效提升了多任务图像检索性能，证明了无需任务标签即可通过伪粒度机制实现跨领域模型适应的可行性。

search_text: Granularity-aware Adaptation for Image Retrieval over Multiple Tasks 多任务图像检索的粒度感知适应 Strong image search models can be learned for a specific domain, ie. set of labels, provided that some labeled images of that domain are available. A practical visual search model, however, should be versatile enough to solve multiple retrieval tasks simultaneously, even if those cover very different specialized domains. Additionally, it should be able to benefit from even unlabeled images from these various retrieval tasks. This is the more practical scenario that we consider in this paper. We address it with the proposed Grappa, an approach that starts from a strong pretrained model, and adapts it to tackle multiple retrieval tasks concurrently, using only unlabeled images from the different task domains. We extend the pretrained model with multiple independently trained sets of adaptors that use pseudo-label sets of different sizes, effectively mimicking different pseudo-granularities. We reconcile all adaptor sets into a single unified model suited for all retrieval tasks by learning fusion layers that we guide by propagating pseudo-granularity attentions across neighbors in the feature space. Results on a benchmark composed of six heterogeneous retrieval tasks show that the unsupervised Grappa model improves the zero-shot performance of a state-of-the-art self-supervised learning model, and in some places reaches or improves over a task label-aware oracle that selects the most fitting pseudo-granularity per task. 可以针对特定领域学习强大的图像搜索模型，即。一组标签，前提是该域的一些标记图像可用。然而，实用的视觉搜索模型应该足够通用，能够同时解决多个检索任务，即使这些任务涵盖非常不同的专业领域。此外，它应该能够从这些不同的检索任务中甚至未标记的图像中受益。这是我们在本文中考虑的更实际的场景。我们使用提出的 Grappa 来解决这个问题，这是一种从强大的预训练模型开始的方法，并对其进行调整以同时处理多个检索任务，仅使用来自不同任务域的未标记图像。我们使用多个独立训练的适配器组扩展了预训练模型，这些适配器组使用不同大小的伪标签集，有效地模仿不同的伪粒度。我们通过学习融合层，将所有适配器集协调成一个适合所有检索任务的统一模型，我们通过在特征空间中的邻居之间传播伪粒度注意力来引导融合层。由六个异构检索任务组成的基准测试结果表明，无监督 Grappa 模型提高了最先进的自监督学习模型的零样本性能，并且在某些地方达到或改进了任务标签感知预言机，该预言机为每个任务选择最合适的伪粒度。 本文提出了一种名为Grappa的方法，通过使用未标记图像和伪粒度注意力机制，使预训练模型能够同时适应多种异构图像检索任务。 Jon Almazán,Byungsoo Ko,Geonmo Gu,Diane Larlus,Yannis Kalantidis cs.CV

authors_text: Paramanand Chandramouli,Kanchana Vaishnavi Gandikota
title: LDEdit: Towards Generalized Text Guided Image Manipulation via Latent Diffusion Models
title_zh: LDEdit：通过潜在扩散模型实现广义文本引导图像操作
comment: Accepted BMVC 2022
comments: Accepted BMVC 2022
abstract: Research in vision-language models has seen rapid developments off-late, enabling natural language-based interfaces for image generation and manipulation. Many existing text guided manipulation techniques are restricted to specific classes of images, and often require fine-tuning to transfer to a different style or domain. Nevertheless, generic image manipulation using a single model with flexible text inputs is highly desirable. Recent work addresses this task by guiding generative models trained on the generic image datasets using pretrained vision-language encoders. While promising, this approach requires expensive optimization for each input. In this work, we propose an optimization-free method for the task of generic image manipulation from text prompts. Our approach exploits recent Latent Diffusion Models (LDM) for text to image generation to achieve zero-shot text guided manipulation. We employ a deterministic forward diffusion in a lower dimensional latent space, and the desired manipulation is achieved by simply providing the target text to condition the reverse diffusion process. We refer to our approach as LDEdit. We demonstrate the applicability of our method on semantic image manipulation and artistic style transfer. Our method can accomplish image manipulation on diverse domains and enables editing multiple attributes in a straightforward fashion. Extensive experiments demonstrate the benefit of our approach over competing baselines.
summary: Research in vision-language models has seen rapid developments off-late, enabling natural language-based interfaces for image generation and manipulation. Many existing text guided manipulation techniques are restricted to specific classes of images, and often require fine-tuning to transfer to a different style or domain. Nevertheless, generic image manipulation using a single model with flexible text inputs is highly desirable. Recent work addresses this task by guiding generative models trained on the generic image datasets using pretrained vision-language encoders. While promising, this approach requires expensive optimization for each input. In this work, we propose an optimization-free method for the task of generic image manipulation from text prompts. Our approach exploits recent Latent Diffusion Models (LDM) for text to image generation to achieve zero-shot text guided manipulation. We employ a deterministic forward diffusion in a lower dimensional latent space, and the desired manipulation is achieved by simply providing the target text to condition the reverse diffusion process. We refer to our approach as LDEdit. We demonstrate the applicability of our method on semantic image manipulation and artistic style transfer. Our method can accomplish image manipulation on diverse domains and enables editing multiple attributes in a straightforward fashion. Extensive experiments demonstrate the benefit of our approach over competing baselines.
abstract_zh: 视觉语言模型的研究近年来取得了快速发展，为图像生成和操作提供了基于自然语言的界面。许多现有的文本引导操作技术仅限于特定类别的图像，并且通常需要微调才能转移到不同的样式或领域。然而，使用具有灵活文本输入的单一模型的通用图像处理是非常可取的。最近的工作通过使用预训练的视觉语言编码器指导在通用图像数据集上训练的生成模型来解决此任务。虽然很有希望，但这种方法需要对每个输入进行昂贵的优化。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种无需优化的方法，用于根据文本提示进行通用图像操作的任务。我们的方法利用最新的潜在扩散模型（LDM）进行文本到图像的生成，以实现零样本文本引导操作。我们在较低维的潜在空间中采用确定性的前向扩散，并且通过简单地提供目标文本来调节反向扩散过程来实现所需的操作。我们将我们的方法称为 LDEdit。我们展示了我们的方法在语义图像处理和艺术风格迁移方面的适用性。我们的方法可以完成不同领域的图像操作，并能够以简单的方式编辑多个属性。大量的实验证明了我们的方法相对于竞争基线的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于潜在扩散模型的无需优化的通用文本引导图像编辑方法LDEdit，实现跨领域和多属性的图像操作。
AI.motivation: 现有文本引导图像操作技术受限于特定图像类别且需微调，优化成本高，亟需一种灵活高效的通用解决方案。
AI.method: 利用潜在扩散模型在低维潜在空间中进行确定性前向扩散，通过目标文本条件反向扩散过程实现零样本图像编辑。
AI.result: 实验表明LDEdit在语义编辑和艺术风格迁移任务中优于现有方法，可处理多领域图像并同时编辑多个属性。
AI.conclusion: LDEdit为通用文本引导图像编辑提供了高效方案，在跨领域应用和多属性编辑方面展现出显著优势。

authors_text: Elvin Johnson,Shreshta Mohan,Alex Gaudio,Asim Smailagic,Christos Faloutsos,Aurélio Campilho
title: HeartSpot: Privatized and Explainable Data Compression for Cardiomegaly Detection
title_zh: HeartSpot：用于心脏肥大检测的私有化且可解释的数据压缩
comment: Accepted to IEEE-EMBS International Conference on Biomedical and Health Informatics 2022. IEEE copyrights may apply
comments: Accepted to IEEE-EMBS International Conference on Biomedical and Health Informatics 2022. IEEE copyrights may apply
abstract: Advances in data-driven deep learning for chest X-ray image analysis underscore the need for explainability, privacy, large datasets and significant computational resources. We frame privacy and explainability as a lossy single-image compression problem to reduce both computational and data requirements without training. For Cardiomegaly detection in chest X-ray images, we propose HeartSpot and four spatial bias priors. HeartSpot priors define how to sample pixels based on domain knowledge from medical literature and from machines. HeartSpot privatizes chest X-ray images by discarding up to 97% of pixels, such as those that reveal the shape of the thoracic cage, bones, small lesions and other sensitive features. HeartSpot priors are ante-hoc explainable and give a human-interpretable image of the preserved spatial features that clearly outlines the heart. HeartSpot offers strong compression, with up to 32x fewer pixels and 11x smaller filesize. Cardiomegaly detectors using HeartSpot are up to 9x faster to train or at least as accurate (up to +.01 AUC ROC) when compared to a baseline DenseNet121. HeartSpot is post-hoc explainable by re-using existing attribution methods without requiring access to the original non-privatized image. In summary, HeartSpot improves speed and accuracy, reduces image size, improves privacy and ensures explainability. Source code: https://www.github.com/adgaudio/HeartSpot

abstract_zh: 用于胸部 X 射线图像分析的数据驱动深度学习的进展强调了对可解释性、隐私、大型数据集和大量计算资源的需求。我们将隐私和可解释性视为有损单图像压缩问题，以在无需训练的情况下减少计算和数据需求。对于胸部 X 射线图像中的心脏肥大检测，我们提出了 HeartSpot 和四个空间偏差先验。 HeartSpot 先验定义了如何根据医学文献和机器的领域知识对像素进行采样。 HeartSpot 通过丢弃高达 97% 的像素（例如那些显示胸廓形状、骨骼、小病变和其他敏感特征的像素）来私有化胸部 X 射线图像。 HeartSpot 先验是事前可解释的，并给出了人类可解释的保留空间特征图像，清晰地勾勒出心脏的轮廓。 HeartSpot 提供强大的压缩功能，像素减少多达 32 倍，文件大小减少 11 倍。与基线 DenseNet121 相比，使用 HeartSpot 的心脏肥大检测器的训练速度提高了 9 倍，或者至少同样准确（高达 +01 AUC ROC）。 HeartSpot 可以通过重新使用现有的归因方法进行事后解释，而无需访问原始的非私有化图像。总之，HeartSpot 提高了速度和准确性，减小了图像大小，提高了隐私性并确保了可解释性。源代码：https://www.github.com/adgaudio/HeartSpot

AI.tldr: 本文提出HeartSpot方法，通过隐私化和可解释的数据压缩技术，用于胸部X光片中的心脏肥大检测。
AI.motivation: 深度学习在胸部X光图像分析中需要可解释性、隐私保护、大规模数据集和计算资源，而现有方法在减少计算和数据需求方面存在不足。
AI.method: 将隐私与可解释性转化为有损单图像压缩问题，提出HeartSpot及四种空间偏差先验，基于医学文献和机器知识采样像素，丢弃97%的敏感信息（如胸廓形状、骨骼等），并实现前向和后向可解释性。
AI.result: HeartSpot压缩率达32倍，文件体积缩小11倍，检测模型训练速度提升9倍，准确率与基准模型相当（AUC ROC提升0.01），且无需原始图像即可复用归因方法进行解释。
AI.conclusion: HeartSpot在保证检测精度的同时，显著降低计算和存储需求，增强隐私保护并确保可解释性，为医疗影像分析提供高效解决方案。

search_text: HeartSpot: Privatized and Explainable Data Compression for Cardiomegaly Detection HeartSpot：用于心脏肥大检测的私有化且可解释的数据压缩 Advances in data-driven deep learning for chest X-ray image analysis underscore the need for explainability, privacy, large datasets and significant computational resources. We frame privacy and explainability as a lossy single-image compression problem to reduce both computational and data requirements without training. For Cardiomegaly detection in chest X-ray images, we propose HeartSpot and four spatial bias priors. HeartSpot priors define how to sample pixels based on domain knowledge from medical literature and from machines. HeartSpot privatizes chest X-ray images by discarding up to 97% of pixels, such as those that reveal the shape of the thoracic cage, bones, small lesions and other sensitive features. HeartSpot priors are ante-hoc explainable and give a human-interpretable image of the preserved spatial features that clearly outlines the heart. HeartSpot offers strong compression, with up to 32x fewer pixels and 11x smaller filesize. Cardiomegaly detectors using HeartSpot are up to 9x faster to train or at least as accurate (up to +.01 AUC ROC) when compared to a baseline DenseNet121. HeartSpot is post-hoc explainable by re-using existing attribution methods without requiring access to the original non-privatized image. In summary, HeartSpot improves speed and accuracy, reduces image size, improves privacy and ensures explainability. authors_text: Valentin Guillet,Dennis G. Wilson,Carlos Aguilar-Melchor,Emmanuel Rachelson
title: On Neural Consolidation for Transfer in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 强化学习中用于迁移的神经巩固
comment: Published at the IEEE Symposium on Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Reinforcement Learning (IEEE ADPRL), 2022
comments: Published at the IEEE Symposium on Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Reinforcement Learning (IEEE ADPRL), 2022
abstract: Although transfer learning is considered to be a milestone in deep reinforcement learning, the mechanisms behind it are still poorly understood. In particular, predicting if knowledge can be transferred between two given tasks is still an unresolved problem. In this work, we explore the use of network distillation as a feature extraction method to better understand the context in which transfer can occur. Notably, we show that distillation does not prevent knowledge transfer, including when transferring from multiple tasks to a new one, and we compare these results with transfer without prior distillation. We focus our work on the Atari benchmark due to the variability between different games, but also to their similarities in terms of visual features.
summary: Although transfer learning is considered to be a milestone in deep reinforcement learning, the mechanisms behind it are still poorly understood. In particular, predicting if knowledge can be transferred between two given tasks is still an unresolved problem. In this work, we explore the use of network distillation as a feature extraction method to better understand the context in which transfer can occur. Notably, we show that distillation does not prevent knowledge transfer, including when transferring from multiple tasks to a new one, and we compare these results with transfer without prior distillation. We focus our work on the Atari benchmark due to the variability between different games, but also to their similarities in terms of visual features.
abstract_zh: 尽管迁移学习被认为是深度强化学习的里程碑，但其背后的机制仍然知之甚少。特别是，预测知识是否可以在两个给定任务之间转移仍然是一个未解决的问题。在这项工作中，我们探索使用网络蒸馏作为特征提取方法，以更好地理解传输发生的上下文。值得注意的是，我们表明蒸馏不会阻止知识转移，包括从多个任务转移到新任务时，并且我们将这些结果与没有事先蒸馏的转移进行比较。由于不同游戏之间的差异性以及视觉特征方面的相似性，我们将工作重点放在 Atari 基准测试上。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了在强化学习中通过神经网络蒸馏实现知识迁移的机制，旨在解决跨任务迁移的预测问题，并在Atari基准测试中验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 尽管迁移学习被视为深度强化学习的重要进展，但其内在机制尚不明确，尤其是如何预测知识在不同任务间的可迁移性仍是一个挑战。
AI.method: 采用网络蒸馏作为特征提取手段，分析其在多任务到新任务迁移中的作用，并与无蒸馏的迁移方法进行对比实验，重点在Atari游戏基准上进行验证。
AI.result: 实验表明，网络蒸馏不会阻碍知识迁移，且在从多个任务向新任务迁移时表现良好，与未使用蒸馏的迁移方法相比具有竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 网络蒸馏能够有效促进强化学习中的知识迁移，尤其在视觉特征相似但任务多样的Atari游戏中，为迁移学习的机制提供了新的见解。

authors_text: Yue Zhu,David Picard
title: Decanus to Legatus: Synthetic training for 2D-3D human pose lifting
title_zh: Decanus 到 Legatus：2D-3D 人体姿势提升的综合训练
comment: Accepted by ACCV 2022
comments: Accepted by ACCV 2022
abstract: 3D human pose estimation is a challenging task because of the difficulty to acquire ground-truth data outside of controlled environments. A number of further issues have been hindering progress in building a universal and robust model for this task, including domain gaps between different datasets, unseen actions between train and test datasets, various hardware settings and high cost of annotation, etc. In this paper, we propose an algorithm to generate infinite 3D synthetic human poses (Legatus) from a 3D pose distribution based on 10 initial handcrafted 3D poses (Decanus) during the training of a 2D to 3D human pose lifter neural network. Our results show that we can achieve 3D pose estimation performance comparable to methods using real data from specialized datasets but in a zero-shot setup, showing the generalization potential of our framework.
summary: 3D human pose estimation is a challenging task because of the difficulty to acquire ground-truth data outside of controlled environments. A number of further issues have been hindering progress in building a universal and robust model for this task, including domain gaps between different datasets, unseen actions between train and test datasets, various hardware settings and high cost of annotation, etc. In this paper, we propose an algorithm to generate infinite 3D synthetic human poses (Legatus) from a 3D pose distribution based on 10 initial handcrafted 3D poses (Decanus) during the training of a 2D to 3D human pose lifter neural network. Our results show that we can achieve 3D pose estimation performance comparable to methods using real data from specialized datasets but in a zero-shot setup, showing the generalization potential of our framework.
abstract_zh: 3D 人体姿态估计是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为很难在受控环境之外获取地面实况数据。许多进一步的问题阻碍了为该任务构建通用且鲁棒的模型的进展，包括不同数据集之间的域差距、训练和测试数据集之间看不见的动作、各种硬件设置和高标注成本等。在本文中，我们提出了一种算法，在 2D 到 3D 人体姿势提升器的训练期间，根据 10 个初始手工制作的 3D 姿势（Decanus）从 3D 姿势分布生成无限 3D 合成人体姿势（Legatus）神经网络。我们的结果表明，我们可以实现与使用来自专业数据集的真实数据的方法相当的 3D 姿态估计性能，但在零样本设置中，显示了我们框架的泛化潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于合成数据的2D到3D人体姿态提升方法，通过生成无限3D姿态数据解决真实数据获取困难的问题。
AI.motivation: 3D人体姿态估计因真实数据获取困难、领域差异、未见过动作及标注成本高等问题，难以构建通用鲁棒模型。
AI.method: 从10个手工设计的3D姿态（Decanus）出发，通过合成生成无限3D姿态数据（Legatus），训练2D-3D姿态提升神经网络。
AI.result: 在零样本设置下，模型性能可媲美使用真实数据的专用数据集方法，验证了框架的泛化潜力。
AI.conclusion: 合成数据训练方法有效缓解了真实数据依赖，为构建通用3D姿态估计模型提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于合成数据的2D到3D人体姿态提升方法，通过生成无限3D姿态数据解决真实数据获取困难的问题。

ai_content.conclusion: 合成数据训练方法有效缓解了真实数据依赖，为构建通用3D姿态估计模型提供了新思路。

search_text: Decanus to Legatus: Synthetic training for 2D-3D human pose lifting Decanus 到 Legatus：2D-3D 人体姿势提升的综合训练 3D human pose estimation is a challenging task because of the difficulty to acquire ground-truth data outside of controlled environments. A number of further issues have been hindering progress in building a universal and robust model for this task, including domain gaps between different datasets, unseen actions between train and test datasets, various hardware settings and high cost of annotation, etc. In this paper, we propose an algorithm to generate infinite 3D synthetic human poses (Legatus) from a 3D pose distribution based on 10 initial handcrafted 3D poses (Decanus) during the training of a 2D to 3D human pose lifter neural network. Our results show that we can achieve 3D pose estimation performance comparable to methods using real data from specialized datasets but in a zero-shot setup, showing the generalization potential of our framework. 3D 人体姿态估计是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为很难在受控环境之外获取地面实况数据。许多进一步的问题阻碍了为该任务构建通用且鲁棒的模型的进展，包括不同数据集之间的域差距、训练和测试数据集之间看不见的动作、各种硬件设置和高标注成本等。在本文中，我们提出了一种算法，在 2D 到 3D 人体姿势提升器的训练期间，根据 10 个初始手工制作的 3D 姿势（Decanus）从 3D 姿势分布生成无限 3D 合成人体姿势（Legatus）神经网络。我们的结果表明，我们可以实现与使用来自专业数据集的真实数据的方法相当的 3D 姿态估计性能，但在零样本设置中，显示了我们框架的泛化潜力。 本文提出一种基于合成数据的2D到3D人体姿态提升方法，通过生成无限3D姿态数据解决真实数据获取困难的问题。 Yue Zhu,David Picard cs.CV

authors_text: Hannes Mareen,Dante Vanden Bussche,Fabrizio Guillaro,Davide Cozzolino,Glenn Van Wallendael,Peter Lambert,Luisa Verdoliva
title: Comprint: Image Forgery Detection and Localization using Compression Fingerprints
title_zh: Comprint：使用压缩指纹进行图像伪造检测和定位
comment: Presented at the Workshop on MultiMedia FORensics in the WILD 2022, held in conjunction with the International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR) 2022
comments: Presented at the Workshop on MultiMedia FORensics in the WILD 2022, held in conjunction with the International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR) 2022
abstract: Manipulation tools that realistically edit images are widely available, making it easy for anyone to create and spread misinformation. In an attempt to fight fake news, forgery detection and localization methods were designed. However, existing methods struggle to accurately reveal manipulations found in images on the internet, i.e., in the wild. That is because the type of forgery is typically unknown, in addition to the tampering traces being damaged by recompression. This paper presents Comprint, a novel forgery detection and localization method based on the compression fingerprint or comprint. It is trained on pristine data only, providing generalization to detect different types of manipulation. Additionally, we propose a fusion of Comprint with the state-of-the-art Noiseprint, which utilizes a complementary camera model fingerprint. We carry out an extensive experimental analysis and demonstrate that Comprint has a high level of accuracy on five evaluation datasets that represent a wide range of manipulation types, mimicking in-the-wild circumstances. Most notably, the proposed fusion significantly outperforms state-of-the-art reference methods. As such, Comprint and the fusion Comprint+Noiseprint represent a promising forensics tool to analyze in-the-wild tampered images.

In an attempt to fight fake news, forgery detection and localization methods were designed. However, existing methods struggle to accurately reveal manipulations found in images on the internet, i.e., in the wild. That is because the type of forgery is typically unknown, in addition to the tampering traces being damaged by recompression. This paper presents Comprint, a novel forgery detection and localization method based on the compression fingerprint or comprint. It is trained on pristine data only, providing generalization to detect different types of manipulation. Additionally, we propose a fusion of Comprint with the state-of-the-art Noiseprint, which utilizes a complementary camera model fingerprint. We carry out an extensive experimental analysis and demonstrate that Comprint has a high level of accuracy on five evaluation datasets that represent a wide range of manipulation types, mimicking in-the-wild circumstances. Most notably, the proposed fusion significantly outperforms state-of-the-art reference methods. As such, Comprint and the fusion Comprint+Noiseprint represent a promising forensics tool to analyze in-the-wild tampered images.
abstract_zh: 真实编辑图像的操作工具随处可见，使任何人都可以轻松创建和传播错误信息。为了打击假新闻，设计了伪造检测和定位方法。然而，现有的方法很难准确地揭示互联网上（即野外）图像中发现的操作。这是因为除了因重新压缩而损坏的篡改痕迹之外，伪造的类型通常是未知的。本文提出了 Comprint，一种基于压缩指纹或 comprint 的新型伪造检测和定位方法。它仅接受原始数据的训练，提供泛化以检测不同类型的操作。此外，我们建议将 Comprint 与最先进的 Noiseprint 相融合，后者利用互补的相机模型指纹。我们进行了广泛的实验分析，并证明 Comprint 在五个评估数据集上具有很高的准确性，这些数据集代表了广泛的操作类型，模仿了野外环境。最值得注意的是，所提出的融合明显优于最先进的参考方法。因此，Comprint 和 Comprint+Noiseprint 的融合代表了一种有前景的取证工具，用于分析野外被篡改的图像。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Comprint方法，通过压缩指纹检测和定位图像伪造，并结合Noiseprint提升效果。
AI.motivation: 现有伪造检测方法难以应对网络环境中未知类型伪造及重压缩导致的痕迹损伤问题。
AI.method: 基于压缩指纹训练模型，仅需原始数据即可泛化检测多种伪造类型，并与Noiseprint融合利用互补特征。
AI.result: 在五个数据集上Comprint准确率高，融合方法显著优于现有技术，尤其在模拟真实场景中表现突出。
AI.conclusion: Comprint及融合方法为分析真实环境下的伪造图像提供了有效工具。

authors_text: Valentin Guillet,Dennis G. Wilson,Carlos Aguilar-Melchor,Emmanuel Rachelson
title: Neural Distillation as a State Representation Bottleneck in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 神经蒸馏作为强化学习中的状态表示瓶颈
comment: Published at the 1st Conference on Lifelong Learning Agents (CoLLAs), 2022
comments: Published at the 1st Conference on Lifelong Learning Agents (CoLLAs), 2022
abstract: Learning a good state representation is a critical skill when dealing with multiple tasks in Reinforcement Learning as it allows for transfer and better generalization between tasks. However, defining what constitute a useful representation is far from simple and there is so far no standard method to find such an encoding. In this paper, we argue that distillation -- a process that aims at imitating a set of given policies with a single neural network -- can be used to learn a state representation displaying favorable characteristics. In this regard, we define three criteria that measure desirable features of a state encoding: the ability to select important variables in the input space, the ability to efficiently separate states according to their corresponding optimal action, and the robustness of the state encoding on new tasks. We first evaluate these criteria and verify the contribution of distillation on state representation on a toy environment based on the standard inverted pendulum problem, before extending our analysis on more complex visual tasks from the Atari and Procgen benchmarks.
summary: Learning a good state representation is a critical skill when dealing with multiple tasks in Reinforcement Learning as it allows for transfer and better generalization between tasks. However, defining what constitute a useful representation is far from simple and there is so far no standard method to find such an encoding. In this paper, we argue that distillation -- a process that aims at imitating a set of given policies with a single neural network -- can be used to learn a state representation displaying favorable characteristics. In this regard, we define three criteria that measure desirable features of a state encoding: the ability to select important variables in the input space, the ability to efficiently separate states according to their corresponding optimal action, and the robustness of the state encoding on new tasks. We first evaluate these criteria and verify the contribution of distillation on state representation on a toy environment based on the standard inverted pendulum problem, before extending our analysis on more complex visual tasks from the Atari and Procgen benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 在强化学习中处理多个任务时，学习良好的状态表示是一项关键技能，因为它允许任务之间的迁移和更好的泛化。然而，定义有用的表示的构成远非简单，而且迄今为止还没有标准方法来找到这种编码。在本文中，我们认为蒸馏（旨在用单个神经网络模仿一组给定策略的过程）可用于学习显示有利特征的状态表示。在这方面，我们定义了三个衡量状态编码所需特征的标准：在输入空间中选择重要变量的能力、根据相应的最佳动作有效分离状态的能力以及状态编码对新任务的鲁棒性。我们首先评估这些标准，并基于标准倒立摆问题验证蒸馏对玩具环境中状态表示的贡献，然后将我们的分析扩展到 Atari 和 Procgen 基准测试中更复杂的视觉任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用神经蒸馏作为强化学习中的状态表示瓶颈，以学习跨任务通用的状态编码。
AI.motivation: 强化学习中多任务状态表示的学习缺乏标准方法，而有效状态表示对任务迁移和泛化至关重要。
AI.method: 定义三个评估标准（变量选择性、动作分离性、任务鲁棒性），通过蒸馏多个策略的神经网络学习状态表示，并在倒立摆、Atari和Procgen任务中验证。
AI.result: 蒸馏方法在玩具环境和视觉任务中均表现出更优的状态表示特性，尤其在新任务中展现更强鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 神经蒸馏能有效学习跨任务的状态表示，为强化学习中的迁移学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Lin Xu,Qixian Zhou,Jinlan Fu,Min-Yen Kan,See-Kiong Ng
title: CorefDiffs: Co-referential and Differential Knowledge Flow in Document Grounded Conversations
title_zh: CorefDiffs：基于文档的对话中的共指和差异知识流
abstract: Knowledge-grounded dialog systems need to incorporate smooth transitions among knowledge selected for generating responses, to ensure that dialog flows naturally. For document-grounded dialog systems, the inter- and intra-document knowledge relations can be used to model such conversational flows. We develop a novel Multi-Document Co-Referential Graph (Coref-MDG) to effectively capture the inter-document relationships based on commonsense and similarity and the intra-document co-referential structures of knowledge segments within the grounding documents. We propose CorefDiffs, a Co-referential and Differential flow management method, to linearize the static Coref-MDG into conversational sequence logic. CorefDiffs performs knowledge selection by accounting for contextual graph structures and the knowledge difference sequences. CorefDiffs significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art by 9.5\%, 7.4\%, and 8.2\% on three public benchmarks. This demonstrates that the effective modeling of co-reference and knowledge difference for dialog flows are critical for transitions in document-grounded conversation
summary: Knowledge-grounded dialog systems need to incorporate smooth transitions among knowledge selected for generating responses, to ensure that dialog flows naturally. For document-grounded dialog systems, the inter- and intra-document knowledge relations can be used to model such conversational flows. We develop a novel Multi-Document Co-Referential Graph (Coref-MDG) to effectively capture the inter-document relationships based on commonsense and similarity and the intra-document co-referential structures of knowledge segments within the grounding documents. We propose CorefDiffs, a Co-referential and Differential flow management method, to linearize the static Coref-MDG into conversational sequence logic. CorefDiffs performs knowledge selection by accounting for contextual graph structures and the knowledge difference sequences. CorefDiffs significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art by 9.5\%, 7.4\%, and 8.2\% on three public benchmarks. This demonstrates that the effective modeling of co-reference and knowledge difference for dialog flows are critical for transitions in document-grounded conversation
abstract_zh: 以知识为基础的对话系统需要在选择用于生成响应的知识之间进行平滑过渡，以确保对话自然流动。对于基于文档的对话系统，文档间和文档内知识关系可用于对此类对话流进行建模。我们开发了一种新颖的多文档共同参照图（Coref-MDG），以有效捕获基于常识和相似性的文档间关系以及基础文档中知识片段的文档内共同参照结构。我们提出了 CorefDiffs，一种共指和差异流管理方法，将静态 Coref-MDG 线性化为会话序列逻辑。 CorefDiffs 通过考虑上下文图结构和知识差异序列来执行知识选择。 CorefDiffs 在三个公共基准测试中明显优于最新技术 9.5%、7.4% 和 8.2%。这表明对话流的共同引用和知识差异的有效建模对于基于文档的对话的转换至关重要

AI.tldr: 本文提出CorefDiffs方法，通过建模共指关系和知识差异序列，提升文档支撑对话系统的知识流连贯性。
AI.motivation: 现有对话系统难以在多文档知识间实现自然过渡，需利用文档内/间的知识关联来优化对话流程。
AI.method: 构建多文档共指图（Coref-MDG）捕捉跨文档常识相似性与文档内共指结构，并提出CorefDiffs方法将静态图结构转化为序列逻辑，结合上下文图与知识差异序列进行知识选择。
AI.result: 在三个基准测试中分别优于现有最优方法9.5%、7.4%和8.2%。
AI.conclusion: 共指关系与知识差异建模对文档支撑对话的流畅性具有关键作用。

search_text: CorefDiffs: Co-referential and Differential Knowledge Flow in Document Grounded Conversations CorefDiffs：基于文档的对话中的共指和差异知识流 Knowledge-grounded dialog systems need to incorporate smooth transitions among knowledge selected for generating responses, to ensure that dialog flows naturally. For document-grounded dialog systems, the inter- and intra-document knowledge relations can be used to model such conversational flows. We develop a novel Multi-Document Co-Referential Graph (Coref-MDG) to effectively capture the inter-document relationships based on commonsense and similarity and the intra-document co-referential structures of knowledge segments within the grounding documents. We propose CorefDiffs, a Co-referential and Differential flow management method, to linearize the static Coref-MDG into conversational sequence logic. CorefDiffs performs knowledge selection by accounting for contextual graph structures and the knowledge difference sequences. CorefDiffs significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art by 9.5\%, 7.4\%, and 8.2\% on three public benchmarks. This demonstrates that the effective modeling of co-reference and knowledge difference for dialog flows are critical for transitions in document-grounded conversation 以知识为基础的对话系统需要在选择用于生成响应的知识之间进行平滑过渡，以确保对话自然流动。对于基于文档的对话系统，文档间和文档内知识关系可用于对此类对话流进行建模。我们开发了一种新颖的多文档共同参照图（Coref-MDG），以有效捕获基于常识和相似性的文档间关系以及基础文档中知识片段的文档内共同参照结构。我们提出了 CorefDiffs，一种共指和差异流管理方法，将静态 Coref-MDG 线性化为会话序列逻辑。 CorefDiffs 通过考虑上下文图结构和知识差异序列来执行知识选择。 CorefDiffs 在三个公共基准测试中明显优于最新技术 9.5%、7.4% 和 8.2%。这表明对话流的共同引用和知识差异的有效建模对于基于文档的对话的转换至关重要 本文提出CorefDiffs方法，通过建模共指关系和知识差异序列，提升文档支撑对话系统的知识流连贯性。 Lin Xu,Qixian Zhou,Jinlan Fu,Min-Yen Kan,See-Kiong Ng cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Josselin Lefèvre,Jean Cousty,Benjamin Perret,Harold Phelippeau
title: Join, select, and insert: efficient out-of-core algorithms for hierarchical segmentation trees
title_zh: 连接、选择和插入：用于分层分割树的高效核外算法
comment: ef:IAPR International Conference on Discrete Geometry and Mathematical Morphology (DGMM), Oct 2022, Strasbourg, France
comments: ef:IAPR International Conference on Discrete Geometry and Mathematical Morphology (DGMM), Oct 2022, Strasbourg, France
abstract: Binary Partition Hierarchies (BPH) and minimum spanning trees are fundamental data structures involved in hierarchical analysis such as quasi-flat zones or watershed. However, classical BPH construction algorithms require to have the whole data in memory, which prevent the processing of large images that cannot fit entirely in the main memory of the computer. To cope with this problem, an algebraic framework leading to a high level calculus was introduced allowing an out-of-core computation of BPHs. This calculus relies on three operations: select, join, and insert. In this article, we introduce three efficient algorithms to perform these operations providing pseudo-code and complexity analysis.
summary: Binary Partition Hierarchies (BPH) and minimum spanning trees are fundamental data structures involved in hierarchical analysis such as quasi-flat zones or watershed. However, classical BPH construction algorithms require to have the whole data in memory, which prevent the processing of large images that cannot fit entirely in the main memory of the computer. To cope with this problem, an algebraic framework leading to a high level calculus was introduced allowing an out-of-core computation of BPHs. This calculus relies on three operations: select, join, and insert. In this article, we introduce three efficient algorithms to perform these operations providing pseudo-code and complexity analysis.
abstract_zh: 二元分区层次结构 (BPH) 和最小生成树是层次分析（例如准平坦区域或分水岭）中涉及的基本数据结构。然而，经典的 BPH 构造算法需要将整个数据存储在内存中，这阻碍了对无法完全放入计算机主内存的大图像的处理。为了解决这个问题，引入了一种高级微积分的代数框架，允许 BPH 的核外计算。该演算依赖于三个操作：选择、连接和插入。在本文中，我们介绍了三种有效的算法来执行这些操作，并提供伪代码和复杂性分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种高效的外部存储算法，用于构建层次分割树，解决大图像处理时内存不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统二元划分层次（BPH）算法需要将整个数据加载到内存中，无法处理超出主存容量的大图像。
AI.method: 引入基于代数框架的高阶计算方法，通过select、join、insert三种操作实现外部存储的BPH构建，并提供伪代码和复杂度分析。
AI.result: 设计了三种高效算法，支持大规模图像的层次分割，降低了内存占用并保持计算效率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过外部存储计算有效解决了大图像处理的内存瓶颈，为层次分割提供了可扩展的解决方案。

authors_text: Simone Bendazzoli,Mehdi Astaraki
title: PriorNet: lesion segmentation in PET-CT including prior tumor appearance information
title_zh: PriorNet：PET-CT 中的病灶分割，包括先前的肿瘤外观信息
abstract: Tumor segmentation in PET-CT images is challenging due to the dual nature of the acquired information: low metabolic information in CT and low spatial resolution in PET. U-Net architecture is the most common and widely recognized approach when developing a fully automatic image segmentation method in the medical field. We proposed a two-step approach, aiming to refine and improve the segmentation performances of tumoral lesions in PET-CT. The first step generates a prior tumor appearance map from the PET-CT volumes, regarded as prior tumor information. The second step, consisting of a standard U-Net, receives the prior tumor appearance map and PET-CT images to generate the lesion mask. We evaluated the method on the 1014 cases available for the AutoPET 2022 challenge, and the results showed an average Dice score of 0.701 on the positive cases.
summary: Tumor segmentation in PET-CT images is challenging due to the dual nature of the acquired information: low metabolic information in CT and low spatial resolution in PET. U-Net architecture is the most common and widely recognized approach when developing a fully automatic image segmentation method in the medical field. We proposed a two-step approach, aiming to refine and improve the segmentation performances of tumoral lesions in PET-CT. The first step generates a prior tumor appearance map from the PET-CT volumes, regarded as prior tumor information. The second step, consisting of a standard U-Net, receives the prior tumor appearance map and PET-CT images to generate the lesion mask. We evaluated the method on the 1014 cases available for the AutoPET 2022 challenge, and the results showed an average Dice score of 0.701 on the positive cases.
abstract_zh: 由于所获取信息的双重性质，PET-CT 图像中的肿瘤分割具有挑战性：CT 中的低代谢信息和 PET 中的低空间分辨率。在医学领域开发全自动图像分割方法时，U-Net架构是最常见和广泛认可的方法。我们提出了一种两步法，旨在细化和提高 PET-CT 中肿瘤病变的分割性能。第一步从 PET-CT 体积生成先前的肿瘤外观图，视为先前的肿瘤信息。第二步由标准 U-Net 组成，接收先前的肿瘤外观图和 PET-CT 图像以生成病变掩模。我们在可用于 AutoPET 2022 挑战的 1014 个病例上评估了该方法，结果显示阳性病例的平均 Dice 得分为 0.701。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PriorNet方法，通过结合PET-CT图像中的先验肿瘤信息，改进肿瘤病变分割效果。
AI.motivation: PET-CT图像中CT代谢信息不足且PET空间分辨率低，导致肿瘤分割难度大，需引入先验信息辅助分割。
AI.method: 采用两步法：首先从PET-CT体积生成先验肿瘤图，再将该图与原始图像输入标准U-Net网络生成病变掩码。
AI.result: 在AutoPET 2022挑战的1014个病例中，阳性病例平均Dice得分为0.701。
AI.conclusion: 结合先验肿瘤信息的PriorNet方法有效提升了PET-CT图像中肿瘤病变的分割性能。

authors_text: Peiwang Tang,Xianchao Zhang
title: MTSMAE: Masked Autoencoders for Multivariate Time-Series Forecasting
title_zh: MTSMAE：用于多元时间序列预测的掩码自动编码器
abstract: Large-scale self-supervised pre-training Transformer architecture have significantly boosted the performance for various tasks in natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision (CV). However, there is a lack of researches on processing multivariate time-series by pre-trained Transformer, and especially, current study on masking time-series for self-supervised learning is still a gap. Different from language and image processing, the information density of time-series increases the difficulty of research. The challenge goes further with the invalidity of the previous patch embedding and mask methods. In this paper, according to the data characteristics of multivariate time-series, a patch embedding method is proposed, and we present an self-supervised pre-training approach based on Masked Autoencoders (MAE), called MTSMAE, which can improve the performance significantly over supervised learning without pre-training. Evaluating our method on several common multivariate time-series datasets from different fields and with different characteristics, experiment results demonstrate that the performance of our method is significantly better than the best method currently available.
summary: Large-scale self-supervised pre-training Transformer architecture have significantly boosted the performance for various tasks in natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision (CV). However, there is a lack of researches on processing multivariate time-series by pre-trained Transformer, and especially, current study on masking time-series for self-supervised learning is still a gap. Different from language and image processing, the information density of time-series increases the difficulty of research. The challenge goes further with the invalidity of the previous patch embedding and mask methods. In this paper, according to the data characteristics of multivariate time-series, a patch embedding method is proposed, and we present an self-supervised pre-training approach based on Masked Autoencoders (MAE), called MTSMAE, which can improve the performance significantly over supervised learning without pre-training. Evaluating our method on several common multivariate time-series datasets from different fields and with different characteristics, experiment results demonstrate that the performance of our method is significantly better than the best method currently available.
abstract_zh: 大规模自监督预训练 Transformer 架构显着提升了自然语言处理 (NLP) 和计算机视觉 (CV) 中各种任务的性能。然而，目前缺乏利用预训练 Transformer 处理多元时间序列的研究，特别是目前针对自监督学习的屏蔽时间序列的研究仍然存在空白。与语言和图像处理不同，时间序列的信息密度增加了研究难度。由于先前的补丁嵌入和掩模方法的无效性，挑战进一步加剧。本文根据多元时间序列的数据特点，提出了一种补丁嵌入方法，并提出了一种基于掩码自动编码器（MAE）的自监督预训练方法，称为MTSMAE，与无预训练的监督学习相比，可以显着提高性能。在来自不同领域、具有不同特征的几个常见的多元时间序列数据集上评估我们的方法，实验结果表明我们的方法的性能明显优于目前可用的最佳方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MTSMAE方法，利用掩码自编码器（MAE）框架改进多变量时间序列预测任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏针对多变量时间序列的预训练Transformer方法，且传统补丁嵌入和掩码策略难以适应时间序列高信息密度特性。
AI.method: 设计面向多变量时间序列的补丁嵌入方法，构建基于掩码自编码器（MAE）的自监督预训练框架MTSMAE。
AI.result: 在多个跨领域多变量时间序列数据集上，MTSMAE性能显著优于当前最优方法及无预训练监督学习模型。
AI.conclusion: MTSMAE验证了自监督预训练在多变量时间序列预测任务中的有效性，为该领域研究提供了新思路。

search_text: MTSMAE: Masked Autoencoders for Multivariate Time-Series Forecasting MTSMAE：用于多元时间序列预测的掩码自动编码器 Large-scale self-supervised pre-training Transformer architecture have significantly boosted the performance for various tasks in natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision (CV). However, there is a lack of researches on processing multivariate time-series by pre-trained Transformer, and especially, current study on masking time-series for self-supervised learning is still a gap. Different from language and image processing, the information density of time-series increases the difficulty of research. The challenge goes further with the invalidity of the previous patch embedding and mask methods. In this paper, according to the data characteristics of multivariate time-series, a patch embedding method is proposed, and we present an self-supervised pre-training approach based on Masked Autoencoders (MAE), called MTSMAE, which can improve the performance significantly over supervised learning without pre-training. Evaluating our method on several common multivariate time-series datasets from different fields and with different characteristics, experiment results demonstrate that the performance of our method is significantly better than the best method currently available. 大规模自监督预训练 Transformer 架构显着提升了自然语言处理 (NLP) 和计算机视觉 (CV) 中各种任务的性能。然而，目前缺乏利用预训练 Transformer 处理多元时间序列的研究，特别是目前针对自监督学习的屏蔽时间序列的研究仍然存在空白。与语言和图像处理不同，时间序列的信息密度增加了研究难度。由于先前的补丁嵌入和掩模方法的无效性，挑战进一步加剧。本文根据多元时间序列的数据特点，提出了一种补丁嵌入方法，并提出了一种基于掩码自动编码器（MAE）的自监督预训练方法，称为MTSMAE，与无预训练的监督学习相比，可以显着提高性能。在来自不同领域、具有不同特征的几个常见的多元时间序列数据集上评估我们的方法，实验结果表明我们的方法的性能明显优于目前可用的最佳方法。 本文提出MTSMAE方法，利用掩码自编码器（MAE）框架改进多变量时间序列预测任务的性能。 Peiwang Tang,Xianchao Zhang cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Jinxin Zhou,Chong You,Xiao Li,Kangning Liu,Sheng Liu,Qing Qu,Zhihui Zhu
title: Are All Losses Created Equal: A Neural Collapse Perspective
title_zh: 所有损失都是平等的吗：神经崩溃的观点
comment: 32 page, 10 figures, NeurIPS 2022
comments: 32 page, 10 figures, NeurIPS 2022
abstract: While cross entropy (CE) is the most commonly used loss to train deep neural networks for classification tasks, many alternative losses have been developed to obtain better empirical performance. Among them, which one is the best to use is still a mystery, because there seem to be multiple factors affecting the answer, such as properties of the dataset, the choice of network architecture, and so on. This paper studies the choice of loss function by examining the last-layer features of deep networks, drawing inspiration from a recent line work showing that the global optimal solution of CE and mean-square-error (MSE) losses exhibits a Neural Collapse phenomenon. That is, for sufficiently large networks trained until convergence, (i) all features of the same class collapse to the corresponding class mean and (ii) the means associated with different classes are in a configuration where their pairwise distances are all equal and maximized. We extend such results and show through global solution and landscape analyses that a broad family of loss functions including commonly used label smoothing (LS) and focal loss (FL) exhibits Neural Collapse. Hence, all relevant losses(i.e., CE, LS, FL, MSE) produce equivalent features on training data. Based on the unconstrained feature model assumption, we provide either the global landscape analysis for LS loss or the local landscape analysis for FL loss and show that the (only!) global minimizers are neural collapse solutions, while all other critical points are strict saddles whose Hessian exhibit negative curvature directions either in the global scope for LS loss or in the local scope for FL loss near the optimal solution. The experiments further show that Neural Collapse features obtained from all relevant losses lead to largely identical performance on test data as well, provided that the network is sufficiently large and trained until convergence.
summary: While cross entropy (CE) is the most commonly used loss to train deep neural networks for classification tasks, many alternative losses have been developed to obtain better empirical performance. Among them, which one is the best to use is still a mystery, because there seem to be multiple factors affecting the answer, such as properties of the dataset, the choice of network architecture, and so on. This paper studies the choice of loss function by examining the last-layer features of deep networks, drawing inspiration from a recent line work showing that the global optimal solution of CE and mean-square-error (MSE) losses exhibits a Neural Collapse phenomenon. That is, for sufficiently large networks trained until convergence, (i) all features of the same class collapse to the corresponding class mean and (ii) the means associated with different classes are in a configuration where their pairwise distances are all equal and maximized. We extend such results and show through global solution and landscape analyses that a broad family of loss functions including commonly used label smoothing (LS) and focal loss (FL) exhibits Neural Collapse. Hence, all relevant losses(i.e., CE, LS, FL, MSE) produce equivalent features on training data. Based on the unconstrained feature model assumption, we provide either the global landscape analysis for LS loss or the local landscape analysis for FL loss and show that the (only!) global minimizers are neural collapse solutions, while all other critical points are strict saddles whose Hessian exhibit negative curvature directions either in the global scope for LS loss or in the local scope for FL loss near the optimal solution. The experiments further show that Neural Collapse features obtained from all relevant losses lead to largely identical performance on test data as well, provided that the network is sufficiently large and trained until convergence.
abstract_zh: 虽然交叉熵（CE）是训练深度神经网络进行分类任务时最常用的损失，但人们已经开发了许多替代损失来获得更好的经验性能。其中，哪一个最好用仍然是一个谜，因为影响答案的因素似乎有多种，比如数据集的属性、网络架构的选择等等。本文通过检查深度网络的最后一层特征来研究损失函数的选择，从最近的一项线路工作中汲取灵感，该工作表明 CE 和均方误差（MSE）损失的全局最优解表现出神经崩溃现象。也就是说，对于训练到收敛为止的足够大的网络，（i）同一类的所有特征都崩溃到相应的类均值，并且（ii）与不同类相关的均值处于其成对距离全部相等且最大化的配置中。我们扩展了这些结果，并通过全局解决方案和景观分析表明，包括常用的标签平滑（LS）和焦点损失（FL）在内的一系列损失函数都表现出神经崩溃。因此，所有相关损失（即 CE、LS、FL、MSE）都会在训练数据上产生相同的特征。基于无约束特征模型假设，我们提供了 LS 损失的全局景观分析或 FL 损失的局部景观分析，并表明（唯一！）全局最小化器是神经崩溃解，而所有其他关键点都是严格鞍点，其 Hessian 在 LS 损失的全局范围内或在接近最优解的 FL 损失的局部范围内表现出负曲率方向。实验进一步表明，只要网络足够大并经过训练直到收敛，从所有相关损失中获得的神经崩溃特征也会在测试数据上产生大致相同的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文从神经崩溃现象的角度研究不同损失函数（如交叉熵、均方误差、标签平滑和焦点损失）在深度网络训练中的等效性。
AI.motivation: 尽管交叉熵损失最常用，但其他损失函数可能在实际中表现更好，然而选择最佳损失函数仍存在争议，因为结果受数据集和网络结构等多因素影响。
AI.method: 通过分析深度网络最后一层特征，结合全局解和损失景观分析，验证包括标签平滑和焦点损失在内的多种损失函数是否表现出神经崩溃现象。
AI.result: 实验表明，在充分训练的大规模网络中，所有相关损失函数（CE、LS、FL、MSE）在训练数据上生成等效的神经崩溃特征，且测试性能高度一致。
AI.conclusion: 不同损失函数在满足神经崩溃条件时生成的特征和性能等效，表明其选择对最终结果影响有限。
